Maa Chitrakrom 03 Sahachari -Confidante
Third in the series inspired by Bengal’s Tenpa Putul .
Folk art often reflects the lives, emotions, and beliefs of ordinary people within a community. In Bengal’s folk traditions — from Tenpa Putul dolls to painted narratives like Ganesh Janani — we repeatedly encounter the image of the mother and son.
But portrayals of mothers and daughters are surprisingly rare.
When they do appear in songs, poems, or rituals, the relationship is often framed around marriage — a daughter leaving home, a mother waiting for her married daughter to return, or the emotional separation between maternal and marital households. We see this tenderness in many cultural narratives, including those surrounding Maa Menoka and her daughter Uma.
Yet we rarely see the quieter journey in between:
a girl growing through childhood into adolescence and womanhood beside her mother.
Through the next works in this series, I want to explore those overlooked moments in a simple folk-inspired visual language — where the daughter is seen not only as a future bride or symbolic goddess, but also as a small human being growing, changing, questioning, and becoming.
In this artwork, I imagine the mother as the girl child’s first confidante. The safest person with whom she shares her fears, discoveries, bodily changes, emotions, and secrets. In return, the mother shares fragments of her own girlhood — stories, memories, warnings, humour, and quiet understanding.
Between them exists a private world of shared experiences and unspoken understanding.

The artwork was created around the vibrant food scene of Bengaluru. A city where cultures, flavours, cafes, street food, brewerie blend into one chaotic rhythm. 

This illustration was created for a series of office wall murals which never saw the light of day because the client stalled the project after the design was done and didn’t even bother to pay the design fees.

Working as a visual artist gives you the opportunity to collaborate with inspiring decision makers. But the other side also teaches you another side of the industry where there are people who think artists’ fee doesn’t deserve consideration.
Moral of the story, keep learning and keep doing what you do best.🙌🏼

Most people think creative careers begin with passion.

Hers began with loneliness.

Sangeeta moved to the US to study Computer Science and worked a corporate job like everyone expected her to.

But after long workdays, she would sit quietly and draw mandalas for hours, not to build a brand, but simply to feel at peace.

Years later, those same drawings became “Miss Compass Hands”, a 7-figure illustration business built completely from scratch.

From learning marketing through YouTube videos…
to packing orders herself…
to building a loyal community through vulnerable storytelling…

her journey proves that sometimes the things we create to heal ourselves end up healing others too.

Today, her work has reached brands like FabIndia, Indian Oil & IIT Jodhpur but the heart behind the brand still remains the same.

One person. One story. One honest beginning. ❤️

#IllustrationArtist #CreativeJourney #WomenWhoCreate #IndianIllustrator #ArtistStory

The SEO game has changed. It's no longer enough to blog about your latest wedding or session. AI has shifted how people and search engines discover you. Today, it's about topical depth where you’re writing content that answers the full conversation your ideal client is already having.

For the past 2+ years I have been quietly experimenting with a new art form. I am certainly not putting down my watercolours, rather, my practice is simply growing another artistic limb.

For those who follow me, you know my deep-seated love of plants. I have been engaging in a new medium that allows me to connect with nature in a completely different way - using sunlight, chemistry, and plant material.

I am thrilled to introduce my new, dedicated Instagram space: 'CYANTRYST' (you can follow me at @cyantryst).

The word itself is a portmanteau. It is a personal 'tryst' (a secret meeting) with 'Cyan' (the colour blue). I also think of it as a noun for the process, or even a title, like a cyan-tist.

It is my new playground where I make impressions of flowers, weeds, seed and leaves onto paper using the cyanotype process. 

If you are curious to see more, I invite you to tap the image and then join me over at @cyantryst.

#Cyantryst #Cyanotype #CyanotypeArt #CyanotypeArtist #SunPrint #PrussianBlue #MumbaiCreative #MumbaiArtists

Let me introduce you to Sharmila Vasudevan, my co-collaborator in our Botanical Cyanotype Workshops.

Sharmila is a dreamer and a true believer in the 'mindful and slow' approach to life - always served with just the right amount of sass. We bond over a shared awe of nature and the animal kingdom, and we are family to each other in every sense of the word.
(Proof of existence: swipe to see a photo of us in a more relaxed setting!)

She is also one of the most well-travelled people I know, wearing her many hats with effortless flair. Sharmila is a voice-over artist, a Tibetan culture enthusiast, a writer, and a dedicated activist. As a lifelong animal lover, she is a devoted adoptive parent to at least six cats at any given time.

In short, there is never a dull moment in Sharmila’s company. She brings this same infectious energy to every one of our workshops, ensuring that your creative journey is as spirited as it is serene.

After two years of immersed play with the Cyanotype medium, I felt ready to share my creative journey on Instagram. I searched for a name... that elusive, perfect word to capture the anticipation and sheer joy that every exposed print brings to me.

After much deliberation, I arrived at 'CYANTRYST'.

The word is a portmanteau that marries the iconic 'cyan' of the cyanotype process with the intimacy of a 'tryst'... a secret meeting between lovers.

One year on and I find it difficult to put into words the magnitude of emotions and experiences we've had since the flood that devastated the Manning Valley - and our home.

We wouldn't be as far along the road to recovery if it weren't for a collective of people - strangers, friends, as well as community organisations and government support.

To everyone from near and far who has been there for us along the way, we cannot thank you enough. We could not have done this alone.

We were able to return home in August last year after three months moving around temporary accommodation, which was challenging in its own ways for the kids and I.

Overall we as a family are doing really well, even if we still have a long way to go.

I have been so focused on flood recovery that many of my creative and life pursuits have been put on the backburner.

Moving forward this page will be more of a focus, while I learn more about my own self (and brain) as a multidisciplinary creative and develop a signature style, artistic practice and my new venture - House of Velvet Ink.

Its a space I wished existed while I was returning to my creativity and artistry following significant trauma and life as a single mum of three.

My brain works in a way that doesn't fit into linear systems and has learned to shrink and adapt in many environments throughout my life.

And while we have entered the age of AI, there is so much this technology will never be able to do.

I've got this feeling multidisciplinary creatives have a skillset  much needed in the world right now.

And when we come together to connect and collaborate, amazing things can happen.

If you'd like to follow the journey, I'd love for you to come along 🌟

Your creativity travels with you. Your instincts travel with you. Your presence travels with you.

Everywhere you show up — all of you shows up too.

“A jack of all trades is a master of none...”

Okay, but can we talk about how everyone always cuts that quote in half? 😅 The full version actually goes: ”...but oftentimes better than a master of one.”

That second part changes everything, doesn’t it?If you’ve ever felt like your brain has 50 different tabs open at the same time, jumping from deep research to soft, creative art, or wanting to learn about so many different things at once, hey, welcome home. You’re not messy or scattered. You’re a multi-hyphenate, and you are exactly who we write, edit, and design for. 🪐✨

Welcome to SN Press! We’re a brand-new, independent publishing house and a literary sanctuary for curious minds. Think of us as your cozy nook, a quiet, safe corner away from the busy world.

We like to think of our books as a bridge between deep thinking and intentional doing. Basically, we take thoughtful ideas about human connection, resilience, and mental wellness, and turn them into beautiful, gentle pages you can hold onto in your everyday life.

Best part? There is absolutely zero pressure here. No “hustle harder” vibes, and no toxic positivity. We believe in books that help you grow at your own human pace without losing your inner peace along the way. 🕊️

We want our nook to feel welcoming to everyone, no matter where you are in the world, what your background is, or how you live your life. If you have a soft soul and a curious mind, there is always a spot saved for you on our shelf.

We are currently working hard behind the scenes putting together our very first titles, so tap that follow button and stay a while. We’re so glad you found us! 🤍

If you're feeling guilty about giving one passion more attention than the others right now — this is for you.

Balance doesn't mean equal time for every passion. Because that's impossible.
It means allowing all of you in — in a way that doesn't make shifting your focus scary or threatening to the rest of you.

Maybe one project or income stream needs you more right now. And the others are waiting. That guilt is real. But focus isn't abandonment.

When one part of your work needs more attention — a launch, a deadline, a new project — it doesn't mean the other parts of you are disappearing. It means you're responding to what's in front of you.

That's not a failure of balance. That's what balance actually looks like.

Focus on what needs you right now. The rest of you isn't going anywhere.

Presenting Artist – Shruti Goenka (Bharat) 
Art Nexus 2026 – Delhi 🇮🇳

My art is a journey inward — a visual exploration of spirituality, mindfulness, emotion, and self-realisation. Each work I create is an attempt to bridge the outer world with the inner self, inviting moments of stillness, reflection, and awareness.

Deeply inspired by my travels — from the monasteries of Ladakh to the ghats of Varanasi, Kedarnath, the temples of South India, and the Kanheri caves of Mumbai — I found a quiet, powerful energy that continues to shape my artistic language. These sacred experiences became the foundation of my practice.

For me, painting is meditation. It is where the mind quiets, and the soul expresses freely — beyond rules, expectations, and material distractions. Through layered textures, symbolic forms, and vibrant colours, I attempt to capture emotions that words often fail to express.

My work often features recurring elements like lotus forms, hand mudras, koi fish, water waves, and expanding circles — symbols of transformation, karma, balance, and continuous growth. Inspired by nature and spiritual philosophy, my art reflects the belief that inner evolution leads to outer harmony.

With over 25 years of artistic journey, 4 solo shows, and participation in 50+ exhibitions and art fairs, my practice extends beyond canvas. Through workshops and art therapy sessions, I explore art as a tool for healing, connection, and self-discovery.

Because true art is not just seen — it is felt.

A bookstore where stories don’t just sit on shelves, but unfold across the space.

The idea was to create an environment where every corner feels like a different world.
From anime and sci-fi to children’s stories and timeless classics, each section was designed to reflect a unique narrative.

This project extended across multiple surfaces.
Walls, columns and even glass panes became part of the canvas.

Over 45 days, the space slowly transformed, layer by layer, into something immersive, expressive and alive.

A project of this scale, valued at ₹1.5L, wasn’t just about painting.
It was about translating stories into visual experiences that people can walk through and feel.

That’s what I love most about murals.
They don’t just decorate a space, they change how you experience it.

If you have a space in mind,

Well, hello there! 👋🐱
I'm Ms Kurious Kitty — and yes, the name is very much on purpose.
IT Consultant by day, which basically means my brain lives in logic and spreadsheets most of the week. But the moment I step away from work? I'm endlessly, relentlessly curious — about the skincare product that actually does what it claims, the food spot nobody's talking about yet, the weekend activity that makes everyone go "wait, THIS exists?!"

Here's how this whole thing started — people kept coming to me for recommendations. Friends, family, colleagues. Always the same questions, always the same conversations. And at some point I thought — why am I repeating myself on WhatsApp at 10pm when I could just write it all down? 😄 So… here we are!

This is my little corner of the internet where I share everything I genuinely try, test, and love — honest beauty reviews, hidden food gems, quirky activities, and wellness finds. Real experience only, never paid opinion. 🌸

A little more about me — I'm a Heartfulness Meditation practitioner 🧘 and a Google Local Guide Level 6 ⭐, which means wellness and finding great places aren't just content for me — they're genuinely how I live.
And I'm proudly Bengaluru-based 🌞 — so if you're dealing with tropical weather, Indian skin concerns, or just looking for something fun to do in or around the city, you've found your person!

It started with, "Who's going to listen to this?" 500 streams and downloads on #spotify later- 5 years to my baby 😌 

-Flashback -
After releasing my book in 2021, I was finding new ways to express myself. And that's how I've always been- learn, invent and reinvent. I truly believe that's how one should be. We should never stop ourselves. 

That curiosity to learn and share whatever I had learned so far gave birth to LET'S TALK AA 🎙️

My main focus was education- mass communication, journalism, films and media...you name it. I love academics, and I love the structure of it. It makes me disciplined and allows me to think beyond normal. Studying mass communication and journalism followed by then Film and screen Media, has been one of the best decisions of my life.

That was something I wanted to talk about. But as I always say, I love to reinvent- or simply pivot- when required. So, I introduced various sections in my podcast: 

talking about books and mass communication with my Dean and colleagues and randomly texting fellow authors and getting the best ones on board, Dil ki baat ( whatever my heart says), introducing my book on Spotify, talking to my cast and crew of MIRA TALKS, and experimenting further by introducing artists from my series WHISPERS OF LIFE. 

Life has been a roller coaster, and with whatever courage I have, I  keep reintroducing myself through my creativity.

-Cut to the Present -

When I received the notification of 500 streams, honestly I had tears in my eyes- because Let's Talk AA is purely made out of love and YOU ALL did this. 

To those who participated, to those who never questioned my vision and trusted me- I can't thank you enough. And if I may say something straight from my heart, I want to thank, that Avleen who trusted her inner voice and went ahead.

I always say, if you do something from your heart, you will meet people who vibrate on the same frequency- and THINGS WILL HAPPEN. Maybe slowly, but they surely will. 

Let's Talk AA is nothing without you guys ♥️

Happy 5th & happy 500 streams to Let's Talk AA ✨

See you soon in season 7 

Love,
Avleen

caption:
Your mind is killing you faster than anything else…
So please guard it.

Every day I interact with so many people who looks fine on the outside, but deep inside constant fight is going in their head.
Thoughts like- 
⤷ Business needs to move fast.
⤷ ROI should cross the target we discussed in our last board meeting.
⤷ Deadline is so near, and we haven’t reached the half. 
⤷ Fed-up with team mates dramas. 
The list can be endless

Fixed mindset keeps reminding that “This shouldn't be happening!"
But the reality speaks out – “Control is just an illusion. Conflict is exhaustion.”

You know most stress we carry on our shoulder usually comes from one mistake.
We keep trying to control what cannot be controlled.
It could be global market, people, our past and our previous outcomes.

The best solution is control what is yours, and kindly release what is not.
Here are the simplest ways to do it in 4 easy steps:

1️⃣ Draw the line
Make habit of writing down the problem in one line. 
Highlight the idea what you can act on today.
2️⃣ Silence the scroll
Doom loops live in feeds. Please stop it for “answers.” 
Limit yourself for reading news to a set time.

3️⃣ 60-minute horizon
Next month planning never exist. 
So, please stop living in next month, and start focus on the next 60 minutes.
4️⃣ One brick momentum
Focus on one thing at a time. It could be one call, one page draft or one deep report analysis. 
Momentum beats panic.
5️⃣End the day with proof
Before going to bed, promise to write your 3 wins, 1 lesson you learnt and 1 priority for tomorrow.
Clarity always helps in killing overthinking.

I have stopped paying attention to what I cannot move. 
That is how I stay sharp.

This weeked, you decide to pick one:
⤷ What's my 60-minute focus tomorrow?
⤷ Which boundary do I enforce first?

Peace isn't absence of pressure. It's presence with what you control.

Traffic signals were built to make life easier for fellow beings. They are the manual for the masses. But for the creative mind, the social code often sounds like gibberish. Most rules are just attempts to fit a blooming brain into a 30km/hr box.

There is no blame in the misalignment—only a signal to build something new.

The strategy is simple: Draft a private constitution. Build a world at home where fear is against the law and rules are reformed until they make sense. There is a specific kind of freedom that comes from being called ‘dumb’ enough to ignore the script and ‘stupid’ enough to take the slow route while the world races by.

The punchline hits when the simulation glitches. The same world that once tried to ‘correct’ the perspective eventually realizes it needs it. It starts signing big checks for the very ‘mistakes’ it labeled as errors.

It’s a cosmic irony. A realization that the signs were always just suggestions for those who didn’t know how to flow.

Still misaligned and the best joke in the city.

In a city of relentless innovation, "pretty" isn't a competitive advantage—clarity is. Most businesses treat design like wallpaper, but at [Your Studio Name], we treat it like architecture.
If you don't have a structure, your design is just decoration. Here is the 3-phase framework we use to build brands that actually scale:
📍 PHASE 1: POSITIONING
We define your market role and competitive contrast. Who are you in the Bangalore ecosystem? We nail down your ideal client definition and set the strategic direction before a single pixel is moved.
🧠 PHASE 2: BRAND CORE
This is the "brain" of your brand. We build your messaging architecture, tone system, and value hierarchy. We ensure your offer structure is built for conversion, not just conversation.
✨ PHASE 3: IDENTITY SYSTEM
Only now do we design. We develop a visual logic—from typography frameworks to color structures—that follows strict application rules. It’s a system designed to work everywhere, from a pitch deck to a billboard on ORR.
Design is the final expression. Not the starting point.

I’ve always leaned toward monotones. Safe. Polished. Predictable.

But this anniversary, I wanted contrast. I wanted conversation. I wanted the color wheel to work overtime.

So I paired deep navy florals with a burst of sunshine yellow and let complementary colors do what they do best: create tension, balance, and magic.

And this hand jewellery? Worn at one of my wedding functions. I was told I’d never wear it again.

Turns out, great pieces don’t retire. They reinvent.

Styling isn’t about buying more. It’s about seeing more possibilities in what you already own.

Would you try high-contrast color blocking or are you still in your monochrome era? 💙💛

Weddings and stories.

The first question I ask someone when they introduce me to their partner - tell me your story. It's no different with every couple I design a wedding card for. In fact, most of the couples want to celebrate their how-we-met-&-fell-in-love story.

Srinidhi got in touch with me through Yashika as well. She had less than a week to go for the wedding and she wanted just one invite. But one that told their love story. And she wanted the card to have a traditional design. With maybe banana plants. 

So I wrote a poem and designed a few options using the same motif. Done and dusted in 2 days. Swipe to see and tell me which one you like of the 5 in the carousel. ➡️
